title: Genipa gummifera
taxonomy: Genipa gummifera is classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically within the order Gentianales. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes coffee, gardenias, and other species. Genipa gummifera's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other members of the Rubiaceae family. Within the Rubiaceae family, Genipa gummifera is also classified as a member of the subfamily Cinchonoideae.
uses: Genipa gummifera is known for its various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used in different cultures to treat various health conditions. Genipa gummifera is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. It is also used to treat respiratory issues like coughs and colds. Additionally, the plant's extracts are being studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
